title: polylepis subtusalbida
intro: Polylepis subtusalbida, also known as the White-barked Willow or Queñua, is a species of tree in the Rosaceae family native to the Andean region of South America. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which contribute to its use in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Polylepis subtusalbida include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive problems, and treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the bark of the tree is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
